title: Fixing React Memoized Component Re-renders on State Change

statement.markdown: You are given a React functional component named `Counter` that displays a counter value. The component uses the `useState` hook to manage its state. When the counter value changes, the component should re-render. However, the current implementation causes the component to re-render unnecessarily when the state changes. Your task is to fix the buggy implementation to prevent unnecessary re-renders.
statement.inputFormat: The input is an object with a `count` property that represents the counter value.
statement.outputFormat: The output is an object with a `count` property that represents the counter value and an `updatedAt` property that represents the timestamp when the counter value was last updated.
statement.notes: The `updatedAt` property should be updated whenever the counter value changes.

examples.explanation: The counter value is 1 and the `updatedAt` property is set to the current timestamp.
The counter value is 2 and the `updatedAt` property is set to the current timestamp.

hints.text: Use the `useMemo` hook to memoize the `updatedAt` property.

editorial.title: Optimizing React Memoized Component Re-renders
editorial.intuition: The `useState` hook updates the state synchronously, causing the component to re-render unnecessarily when the state changes. We can use the `useMemo` hook to memoize the `updatedAt` property and update it only when the counter value changes.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach is to re-render the component whenever the state changes, which can be inefficient.
editorial.betterSolution: We can use the `useMemo` hook to memoize the `updatedAt` property and update it only when the counter value changes.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use the `useMemo` hook to memoize the `updatedAt` property and update it only when the counter value changes.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness is that the `updatedAt` property is updated only when the counter value changes, preventing unnecessary re-renders.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: The component is re-rendered when the state changes, causing the `updatedAt` property to be updated.
